text.title: Spring Async Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Self-invocation, default executor hazard, Transactional propagation loss, virtual-thread upgrade trap.

antipattern.description: Calling an @Async method from the same bean instance: the method runs synchronously with no proxy interception and no error.
antipattern.reason: Spring AOP creates a proxy around the bean; internal this.method() calls bypass the proxy. Extract the async method to a separate Spring bean to ensure the proxy intercepts the call.

antipattern.description: Using plain @Async without a named executor in a Spring Boot 3.2+ application with virtual threads enabled.
antipattern.reason: Boot 3.2+ with spring.threads.virtual.enabled=true switches the default executor to a virtual-thread SimpleAsyncTaskExecutor. Existing pool-size tuning is silently ignored; behavior changes on upgrade.

antipattern.description: Converting a @Transactional method to @Async without a human checkpoint.
antipattern.reason: @Async spawns a new thread; the outer @Transactional does not propagate to it. The async method runs outside the transaction, causing silent commit/rollback divergence from the caller's expectations.

antipattern.description: Using Thread.sleep in async tests: sleep duration is guessed, flaky under CI load.
antipattern.reason: CI machines vary in CPU availability. A 2-second sleep that passes locally fails when the CI node is busy. Awaitility.await().atMost() polls the actual condition and terminates as soon as it is true.

antipattern.description: Suggesting @Async to parallelize CPU-heavy computation (e.g., image processing, ML inference).
antipattern.reason: ThreadPoolTaskExecutor is sized for IO-bound work where threads spend most time waiting. CPU-bound tasks need ForkJoinPool or Project Reactor's parallel scheduler, which maps work to actual core counts.

antipattern.description: Missing MDC propagation: async threads log without the request-id or user-id context set by the caller's MDC.
antipattern.reason: MDC is thread-local; it does not transfer to the async thread automatically. A TaskDecorator that copies and restores MDC entries must be registered on the executor.
